pageIntro: A curated collection of investigative deep-dives — video case files chronicling the decisions that shaped the modern world.
emptyState.title: New Dossiers in Production
emptyState.body: The archive is being filmed. The first video dossiers will publish here shortly — check back soon.
openInNewTab: opens on YouTube in a new tab
lincoln.title: Democracy Under Fire: Lincoln and the 1864 Election
lincoln.summary: With Grant's army bogged down in Virginia and calls for a negotiated peace growing louder, Abraham Lincoln wrote a private memorandum acknowledging probable defeat. He made his cabinet sign it sight unseen. Then he held the election anyway — refusing to suspend the democratic process even as it threatened to end his presidency and, with it, the Union cause.
lincoln.imageAlt: Candlelit desk with a sealed document, quill, and inkwell evoking Lincoln's private 1864 memorandum
sedition.title: When Free Speech Became a Crime: The 1798 Panic
sedition.summary: In a young republic on the brink of war, criticizing the President became a federal offense. Explore the dramatic trials of journalists and veterans caught in the crosshairs of America's first great battle over the First Amendment.
sedition.imageAlt: Quill, gavel, and folded broadside newspaper on a candlelit desk evoking a late-eighteenth-century sedition trial
homestead.title: The Homestead Massacre: When Titans Fought for Control
homestead.summary: The violent collision between Andrew Carnegie's steel empire and the workers who built it — a study in corporate dominance and industrial warfare.
homestead.imageAlt: Industrial-era steel mill at dusk, heavy smoke silhouetting worker figures
frenchConnection.title: The French Connection: The Secret Loans of 1776
frenchConnection.summary: Beyond the battlefield, the American Revolution was fought in the counting houses of Paris. Discover the shadow financiers who wagered everything on a rebel nation.
frenchConnection.imageAlt: Ancient scrolls and wax-sealed letters on a mahogany desk with candlelight
blackTuesday.title: The Day the Bull Died: Re-examining Black Tuesday
blackTuesday.summary: Not just a market crash — a systemic failure of confidence. We trace the three days that erased a generation's wealth and rewrote the role of government.
blackTuesday.imageAlt: Sepia photograph of a 1929 Wall Street crowd amid falling newspaper headlines
louisiana.title: The Great Land Gamble: Louisiana Re-Investigated
louisiana.summary: Jefferson called it a noble bargain. Napoleon called it a necessary loss. A forensic look at the largest real-estate deal in history.
louisiana.imageAlt: Wide prairie at golden hour with a distant steam train crossing the horizon
telegraph.title: The First Tweet: How the Telegraph Broke the World
telegraph.summary: Samuel Morse did not just invent a machine — he invented instantaneous news. How the first data network rewrote the speed of politics.
telegraph.imageAlt: Close-up of an antique printing press with fresh ink on a telegraph bulletin
